The S26 Ultra is the top-end model in the lineup, and one of its headline features is the Privacy Display. The Privacy Display is advertised as a zero-peek feature that makes it hard …

title: OpenAI's massive Stargate data center canceled as firm can't reach terms with Oracle, operator struggles with reliability issues — Meta said to be interested in snatching excess capacity
summary: Financing terms and swinging OpenAI capacity forecast collapse a major expansion of a Stargate data center. Yet, Meta could take the yet-to-be-expanded space.

title: Samson's powerful display puts India on track for huge score
summary: Sanju Samson makes 89 runs off 46 balls, including eight sixes, to put India in the commanding position of 203-2 with five overs to go in their ICC Men's T20 World Cup final against New Zealand.

title: Car powered by 500 disposable vape batteries boasts 18-mile range, 35mph top speed, USB-C charging port — early 2000s Reva G.Wiz gets a makeover
summary: A TechTuber has successfully built and tested a vape battery-powered car.

title: NASA successfully deflected a small asteroid with its DART rocket, kinetic strike ejected a significant amount of debris from the asteroid — impact nudges the orbit of the small astronomical body, affecting the larger cosmic object as well
summary: The U.S. space agency measured the impact of its DART mission, revealing that the impact changed the orbit of two cosmic bodies.

title: Palmer Luckey’s retro gaming venture seeks $1 billion valuation as FPGA-based Nintendo 64 clone launch nears — ModRetro's M64 console plays original N64 cartridges, supports 4K resolution
summary: Palmer Luckey is said to be in talks with investors to raise funds for ModRetro at a $1 billion valuation as the retro gaming company prepares to ship its second product.

title: Chinese SSD maker YMTC lists its first commercial PCIe 5.0 SSD as worldwide shortage intensifies — Xtacking 4.0 NAND powers speeds of up to 10,500 MB/s
summary: The newly listed PC550 SSD targets AI PCs and commercial systems and features PCIe 5.0 x4 connectivity, NVMe 2.0 support, and a four-channel design.

title: Two-time Olympian Perathoner wins Paralympic gold
summary: Snowboarder Emanuel Perathoner competed at two Olympics for Italy. After a devastating knee injury, and then a move into Para-sport, he has won Paralympic gold at his home Games.

title: US startup plans to build data centers inside ocean-based wind turbines, servers water cooled via chilly North Sea — each leg houses a data center, firm set to launch three-legged prototype in Norway’s North Sea this year
summary: Offshore wind turbines could house up to a 12-megawatt data center, drawing power directly from the winds blowing on the sea while using the cold ocean water for cooling.

title: Mercedes 'hit ground running' with new F1 car regulations
summary: On the latest episode of The Chequered Flag Podcast, former McLaren mechanic Marc Priestly credits Mercedes for the way they have managed to "hit the ground running" on integrating new F1 car regulations.

title: Norwegian gov't consumer watchdog calls out ‘enshittification’ of video games, connected devices, and others — claims hardware deliberately degraded after purchase
summary: “Companies can degrade the functionality of your car or effectively destroy your connected washing machine with a software update,” says the report.

title: Jensen Huang set to receive $4 million bonus from Nvidia, 0.002% of his $164 billion net worth — a small incentive for one of the richest people on earth
summary: The Nvidia compensation committee set a $4 million cash bonus for Jensen Huang if he hits the company's fiscal targets by January 31, 2027.

title: 'England's style of play is tedious and doesn't allow players to express themselves'
summary: England World Cup winner Matt Dawson discusses their historic first loss to Italy and why they need to play with more freedom against France.

title: Was new-era F1 really racing or more like a computer game?
summary: The opening race of F1's new era in Australia left the drivers with mixed feelings about what the sport had become, writes Andrew Benson.

title: Seagate FireCuda X1070 SSD spotted at retailers — listed at $829.99 before any official announcement
summary: Seagate's unannounced FireCuda X1070 NVMe SSD appeared on Amazon and Best Buy listings this week before its product page went offline.

title: California sues websites hosting 3D printed gun files — online platforms allegedly violate multiple civil codes regarding unlawful distribution and manufacturing of firearms
summary: California's civil suit alleges that these websites and their operators are willfully distributing 3D printed gun files and instructions that could allow anyone to print a gun from scratch in as little as eight hours.

title: The anatomy of a corner-kick: how set-pieces reshaped the Premier League
summary: As corner kicks continue to cause chaos in the Premier League, BBC Sport take a closer at why they are becoming so hard to defend.

title: Memory bit flips cause up to 15% of Firefox crashes, asserts Mozilla engineer — figure inferred from 470,000 auto-submitted crash reports
summary: A Mozilla engineer has shared survey data and calculations suggesting that up to 15% of Firefox crashes are due to a bit flip.

title: 'When you're with me, you're not bored' - Malard bringing sunshine to Man Utd
summary: BBC Sport meets Manchester United and France forward Melvine Malard to discover her story.

title: Marmoush double fires Man City past Newcastle
summary: Omar Marmoush scores twice against Newcastle United for the second time this season as Manchester City come from behind to reach the FA Cup quarter-finals.

title: Linux hacked onto a PS5 to turn Sony's console into a Steam Machine — GTA V Enhanced Edition runs at 60 FPS on 1440pwith ray tracing
summary: If Sony's allegedly going to take away the ability to play its PlayStation exclusives on PC, you can rebel by converting the PS5 into a straight-up PC. That's what Andy Nguyen, a security engineer, did by porting Linux over to the console and running GTA V on it via Steam. The best part? It actuall…

title: Lock in the RTX 5070 for $599 before prices skyrocket even further — custom overclocked model is only $50 above MSRP
summary: The RTX 5070 offers strong 1440p performance, 12GB of GDDR7 memory, and support for Nvidia’s latest features like DLSS 4 and Reflex 2.

title: Valve changes Steam Machine release date to this 'year,' second change as AI-fueled memory and shortage crisis deepens — official announcements went from "early 2026" to "first half of 2026" to "this year"
summary: Valve's latest announcement puts the Steam Machine release for "this year," after changing the console's expected arrival date from the first quarter to the first half of 2026.

title: AMD VP uses AI to create Radeon Linux userland driver in Python — senior AI engineer says he "didn't open the editor once"
summary: AMD's VP of AI software vibe coded the driver entirely using Claude Code, but it's meant for testing, not for deployment to users.

title: Engineer receives $30,000 for exposing a vulnerability affecting 7,000 robot vacuum cleaners — tinkerer just wanted to drive his robot vacuum with a PS5 controller
summary: A software engineer gets access to 7,000 DJI Romo robot vacuum cleaners while tinkering with an idea to use a gamepad to control his robotic hoover. Gets $30,000 from DJI for an undisclosed discovery.

title: Oracle reportedly set to axe thousands of jobs and freeze hiring as AI data center bets ignite financial perfect storm —  cuts in cloud division to be backfilled with AI
summary: Oracle reportedly set to axe thousands of jobs, could freeze hiring for cloud division

title: Scientists attempt to link 3D printed ghost guns to specific filament brands with chemical fingerprinting — major filament makers often white-label products, complicating efforts
summary: A new study investigates whether science can trace 3D printed “ghost guns” through chemical analysis of the filament used for printing.

title: Claude Code deletes developers' production setup, including its database and snapshots — 2.5 years of records were nuked in an instant
summary: Over-eager developer has Claude Code Terraform his production setup, including database and its snapshots

title: Nvidia dominates gaming GPU market with 95 percent share as sales of AMD Radeon graphics plummet to a historical low of 5 percent
summary: Nvidia strengthens its position on the market of discrete GPUs as AMD's market share continues to dive, whereas Intel fails to gain traction.

title: Nvidia CEO Huang declares ‘I love constraints’ amid ongoing component shortage — claims lack of options forces AI clients to only choose the very best
summary: In a scarce market, clients don't take risks and go with the biggest, most reliable option, which ultimately benefits Nvidia as it sits at the top of the AI boom. Unfortunately, these data center escapades hurt regular human beings, too, but that's not the trillion-dollar corporation's concern.

title: This shark PC case will take a $5,499 megabyte out of your pocket — Cooler Master Shark X finally migrates to U.S. waters
summary: Cooler Master's Shark X mini-ITX case is finally available at U.S. retailers for $5,499.99.

title: How safe is bare-knuckle boxing as it fights for mainstream appeal?
summary: A new BBC documentary looks at the rise of bare-knuckle boxing as fighter Liam 'Rocky' Rees tells BBC Sport why he loves the adrenaline.

title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
summary: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

title: 'I've been flying through the air without skis on - that is a weird feeling'
summary: What do you do if you fall 40 feet on to hard-packed snow? If you're a Winter Olympics athlete, you pick yourself up and try again.

title: Happy tennis, serious name - how history-maker Alcaraz clinched career Grand Slam
summary: Before Carlos Alcaraz became the youngest man to complete the career Grand Slam, he was simply Carlitos from Murcia. This is his story.

title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
summary: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

title: Rejections, depression & promise to his mum - Thiago's route to top
summary: Igor Thiago's route to the Premier League has been far from straightforward - BBC Sport charts the rise of Brentford's record-breaking Brazilian striker.
